title: Staff Nurse (Healthcare Support)
description:
Responsibilities:


Nursing Assessment, developing individual care plan, monitoring and evaluation

  • Assess, monitor, review care needs of client and caregiver.
  • Develop individualised care plan and identify types, duration and frequency of services.
  • Monitor client's response to services and evaluate progress towards goals.
  • Review clients care plan 6 monthly and whenever necessary.


Nursing intervention and treatment plan

  • Schedule times to visit for follow-ups, and remain in contact with the client and/or family throughout the treatment process
  • In carrying out the treatment plan the home care nurse is responsible for the following:
  • Manage acute and chronic medical conditions in consultation with a doctor
  • Perform nursing procedures such as wound care, tube change, medication management and initiate medical care interventions where necessary
  • Provide psychological and spiritual support
  • Provide health education for the client and his caregiver
  • Provide caregiver’s training for the client’s caregiver
  • Maintain updated case records of clients
  • Supervises any delegated task to junior staff
  • Coordinate referrals, provide health and social education


Other Duties

  • Assist and support with administration processes and paperwork when needed
  • Any other general duties as required


Requirements:


Qualifications and Registrations:

  • Possess a Diploma or Degree in Nursing.
  • Must hold a valid Practising Certificate and be a Registered Nurse (RN) with the Singapore Nursing Board (SNB).
  • Valid Basic Cardiac Life Support (BCLS) and Automated External Defibrillator (AED) certification
  • Preferred: Advanced/Post-basic Diploma in a relevant field such as Geriatrics, Community Health, Chronic Disease Management, or Palliative Care.

Experience:

  • Minimum of 3 years of clinical experience in an acute care setting (e.g., medical/surgical ward) or community/geriatric setting is typically preferred.
  • Experience in performing a range of technical nursing procedures independently.
  • Familiarity with the Singapore Community Care sector and resources is an advantage.

Skills and Attributes:

  • Ability to work independently and manage a caseload of patients on home visits.
  • Excellent clinical judgment and critical thinking skills for assessment and intervention.
  • Strong interpersonal and communication skills (written and verbal) to build rapport with patients, caregivers, and external partners.
  • High level of adaptability and resilience to work in various home environments and handle unexpected situations.
  • Proficiency in English and often a second language/dialect (e.g., Mandarin, Malay, or Tamil) to communicate effectively with the local elderly population.


If you are interested to join us, please apply via Fei Yue Career Portal.


For your privacy and security, kindly refrain from including your NRIC number in your application form or resume. Please note that we do not accept hardcopy resumes.

postedCompany.description: Fei Yue Community Services is a  non-profit voluntary welfare organization established in 1996, with a mission to effect life transformation through the provision of quality social services. We are committed to serving and reaching out to the needy in Singapore through our diverse services and programmes. We believe in promoting social development among all ages, seek to inculcate the spirit of volunteerism in the community, and advocate strong family ties in the society. Our services and programmes, which are varied and cater to the needs of different client populations, include counselling, adoption, family life education,  early intervention programmes for infants and children, and services for youths, elderly, inmates and their families.
postedCompany.name: Fei Yue Community Services
